text:p.text:span: Right-click the TOC and select "Update Field" to refresh page numbers

office:text.text:p: This document presents an independent technical audit of the Valenze project (github.com/crvlinares/Valenze), a Telegram-based conversational finance assistant. The audit was conducted on June 4, 2026, analyzing the complete source code, architecture, security posture, and infrastructure configuration.

text:p.text:span: The project has SEVERE security vulnerabilities that require immediate remediation before any production deployment. Hardcoded API tokens, exposed database credentials, and disabled Row Level Security represent critical risks.
• Source code: JavaScript files (botLogic.js, db.js, parser.js, index.js, hotelBotLogic.js)
• Serverless API endpoints: api/webhook.js, api/cron.js, api/hotel-webhook.js
No tests, no linting, no types

office:text.text:p: The security analysis reveals multiple critical vulnerabilities that expose the application to unauthorized access, data breaches, and potential abuse of Telegram Bot API resources.

text:p.text:span: A Telegram Bot token is hardcoded in api/hotel-webhook.js: '8521262565:AAG7eoeXZln_ST_mmRku8Fx65zPelcc-2xQ'. This is a production credential committed to version control, permanently exposing it to anyone with repository access. The token must be rotated immediately and removed from git history.
• Cron secret hardcoded as 'valanze2026' in both vercel.json and api/cron.js
• Admin command password '/admin_stats valanze2026' hardcoded in botLogic.js
• No use of environment variables for the hotel bot webhook token

Row Level Security (RLS) is effectively disabled. The schema.sql creates a policy 'Permitir acceso total al bot de Telegram' with USING (true) AND WITH CHECK (true), granting unlimited read/write access to any anonymous user. The documentation even suggests using the service_role key, which bypasses RLS entirely.
• No user authentication beyond telegram_id — any user knowing another's Telegram ID can access their financial data
• The RLS policy allows ALL operations to the anon role without any restrictions
• No rate limiting on database operations
• Financial data (amounts, descriptions, transaction history) stored without encryption
• No webhook secret token validation — the webhook endpoint processes any incoming POST request without verifying it originates from Telegram
• No rate limiting on message processing — susceptible to spam/denial of service
• No input sanitization beyond basic regex parsing — potential for injection attacks
• Admin stats command uses a trivial password that is publicly visible in source code

office:text.text:p: The codebase represents an early-stage MVP with significant architectural and quality concerns that will impede scaling and maintenance.

text:p.text:span: • Monolithic architecture with no separation of concerns — business logic, data access, and presentation mixed in single files
• No test suite whatsoever — zero unit tests, integration tests, or end-to-end tests
• No linting configuration (ESLint) or code formatting (Prettier)
• No TypeScript — JavaScript without static typing increases bug risk in financial calculations
• Only 8 commits in repository history — extremely limited development traceability

office:text.text:p: The message parser (parser.js) uses a single regex to extract amounts and descriptions. While functional for simple cases, it has significant limitations:

text:p.text:span: • No support for multiple currencies — hardcoded to Peruvian Sol (S/)
• Limited keyword list for income detection (8 keywords) — not extensible without code changes
• No categorization system — transactions are stored as free-text descriptions
• Edge cases not handled: '2x15 menu', '15.50+10 propina', decimal edge cases
• No machine learning or NLP despite being mentioned in the roadmap
• Generic try/catch blocks with console.error only — no structured logging
• No retry mechanisms for failed database operations
• No graceful degradation — if Supabase is down, the bot simply crashes or returns generic error messages
• No error tracking service (Sentry, Rollbar) integrated
• Vercel serverless functions have execution time limits — the cron job iterates over ALL users without pagination, risking timeouts at scale
• Cold starts on Vercel Hobby tier may cause delayed responses
• No queuing mechanism for high-volume message processing
• Webhook endpoint returns 200 OK before fully processing the message — no delivery guarantees
• Database queries lack pagination — getBalance() loads all user transactions into memory
• No caching layer (Redis) for frequently accessed data like user balances
• No connection pooling configuration for Supabase
• The cron job sends messages sequentially in a loop — with 1000+ users, this will exceed serverless timeout limits
• No alerting for failures or anomalies
• Core functionality: basic transaction logging and balance viewing only
• No categorization or analytics — users cannot see spending patterns
• No web dashboard (promised in roadmap but not implemented)
• The '/deshacer' (undo) command is basic — deletes the last transaction without confirmation dialog
• No user onboarding flow beyond a static /start message

office:text.text:p: The hotelBotLogic.js and api/hotel-webhook.js implement a completely separate demo bot for hostel operations. However:

text:p.text:span: • It is a mock/stub with no real database integration — all responses are hardcoded strings
• Shares the same repository but serves a different use case, creating confusion
• The hardcoded Telegram token in this file is a critical security breach
• No clear path from this demo to a real B2B product
• Freemium model proposed ($2.70/month) is optimistic given the extremely basic current feature set
• Competitive landscape (YNAB, Mint, Splitwise, etc.) offers significantly more features for free

office:text.text:p: The project's own TECHNICAL_AUDIT.md makes several claims that do not align with the actual codebase:

text:p.text:span: Webhook Authentication validates Telegram secret tokens
Stateless compute, no data in memory

office:text.text:p: This discrepancy between documented capabilities and actual implementation is concerning for potential investors or technical partners evaluating the project.
The following actions are recommended in order of priority:

text:list-item.text:p: Rotate the exposed Telegram Bot token immediately and remove it from git history using git-filter-repo or BFG Repo-Cleaner
Move ALL secrets to environment variables — never commit credentials to version control
Implement proper Row Level Security policies that restrict access per telegram_id
Add webhook secret token validation to verify requests originate from Telegram
Implement rate limiting on all endpoints
Add input validation and sanitization for all user messages
Implement structured logging with a service like Winston or Pino
Add basic error tracking (Sentry free tier)
Write unit tests for the parser module (cover edge cases)
Add ESLint and Prettier configuration for code consistency
Migrate to TypeScript for type safety, especially for financial calculations
Implement a transaction categorization system (even rule-based initially)
Add database query pagination for the cron job and balance queries
Implement a proper undo mechanism with confirmation and audit trail
Create the web dashboard (promised in roadmap and TECHNICAL_AUDIT.md)
Separate the hotel demo into its own repository
Consider migrating from node-telegram-bot-api to a more modern framework like Telegraf or Grammy
Implement proper CI/CD pipeline with automated testing
Add multi-currency support
Build the premium features (voice notes, Excel export, advanced analytics) before attempting monetization
Conduct a proper security audit by an external firm before handling real user financial data

text:p.text:span: Immediate action required on security findings
